option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"D:/PROJECTS/PYTHON/academia":"{\"/templates/attendance_report_form.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that displays a report of attendance requests. It does not contain any classes, but it does have the following functions:\\n\\n`setOutputFormat`: This function sets the value of the `output_format` hidden input field to the specified format (either \\\"pdf\\\" or \\\"xlsx\\\") when the user clicks the corresponding button.\\n\\nThe file also includes the following HTML elements and functionality:\\n\\n- A form that allows the user to filter the attendance report by start date, end date, class, and status.\\n- A table that displays the filtered attendance data, including the student\\u0027s name, class, attendance date, and status.\\n- Buttons to generate the report in PDF or XLSX format.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/index.html\":\"This file is a Django template that serves as the homepage for an academia (gym) management system. It extends the base template and includes the following functionality:\\n\\n1. If the user is not authenticated, it displays a hero section with a logo, title, and buttons to log in or request access.\\n2. It displays a features section with cards highlighting the key features of the system, such as attendance management, class management, and grading system.\\n3. If the user is authenticated, it displays a welcome message with the user\\u0027s name and a button to access the dashboard.\\n4. It provides quick access links for students to mark attendance, view their attendance records, and access reports.\\n5. It provides quick access links for professors or administrators to manage classes, approve attendance, manage graduations, and access reports.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a template that renders the appropriate content based on the user\\u0027s authentication status.\",\"/academia/forms.py\":\"`GraduacaoForm`: A Django form for creating and updating Graduacao model instances.\\n\\n`ItemForm`: A Django form for creating and updating Item model instances.\\n\\n`PedidoForm`: A Django form for creating and updating Pedido model instances.\\n\\nThis file defines three Django forms: `GraduacaoForm`, `ItemForm`, and `PedidoForm`. These forms are used to create and update instances of the `Graduacao`, `Item`, and `Pedido` models, respectively. The forms use Django\\u0027s built-in `forms.ModelForm` to automatically generate the necessary form fields based on the model definitions.\",\"/academia/auth_backends.py\":\"`CustomAuthBackend`: A custom authentication backend that extends the `ModelBackend` class to authenticate users based on email, password, and access group.\\n\\n`authenticate`: A method that authenticates a user based on the provided email, password, and access group, and returns the user object if the authentication is successful.\\n\\n`get_user`: A method that retrieves a user object based on the provided user ID.\\n\\nThis file defines a custom authentication backend for a Django application, which allows users to be authenticated based on their email, password, and access group. The `CustomAuthBackend` class overrides the `authenticate` and `get_user` methods of the `ModelBackend` class to implement the custom authentication logic.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/rankings.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a list of rankings for a Jiu-Jitsu academy. It extends the base template and includes a block for the content. The template checks if there are any rankings and displays them in a table, with options to create a new ranking and navigate back to the dashboard.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/solicitar_acesso.html\":\"This file is an HTML document that provides a form for users to request access to a Jiu-Jitsu academy\\u0027s system. It uses Bootstrap for styling and includes JavaScript code to validate the password confirmation field and the form submission.\\n\\nThere are no classes defined in this file. The following functions are present:\\n\\n`addEventListener(\\u0027input\\u0027, function)`: Validates the password confirmation field to ensure that the password and confirmation match.\\n\\n`addEventListener(\\u0027submit\\u0027, function)`: Validates the password and confirmation fields before submitting the form.\",\"/jiujitsu_academy/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided file. The file appears to be an empty XML document with a `\\u003cdocument\\u003e` tag, but no content within it.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/aluno/relatorios.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template for a report page in an academia (gym) application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the structure and content of the page. The main functionality of this file is:\\n\\n1. Displaying a form to select the report type (attendance) and the date range (current month or custom dates).\\n2. Displaying summary statistics (total, approved, rejected, and pending) based on the selected report.\\n3. Displaying a table of attendance records with details such as date, class, and status.\\n4. Providing options to download the report in PDF or XLSX format.\\n5. Handling the dynamic display of the date range input fields based on the selected period.\",\"/academia/admin.py\":\"This file defines the Django admin configuration for various models in a web application. Here\\u0027s a summary of each class and function:\\n\\n`UserAdmin`: Customizes the Django admin interface for the `User` model, adding additional fields and filtering options.\\n\\n`TurmaAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `Turma` (class) model, allowing management of class information.\\n\\n`TurmaAlunoAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `TurmaAluno` (class student) model, allowing management of student enrollment in classes.\\n\\n`PresencaAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `Presenca` (attendance) model, allowing management of student attendance records.\\n\\n`GraduacaoAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `Graduacao` (graduation) model, allowing management of student graduation information.\\n\\n`PlanoAulaAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `PlanoAula` (lesson plan) model, allowing management of lesson plans.\\n\\n`ItemPlanoAulaAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `ItemPlanoAula` (lesson plan item) model, allowing management of individual lesson plan items.\\n\\n`RankingAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `Ranking` model, allowing management of student rankings.\\n\\n`PosicaoRankingAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `PosicaoRanking` (ranking position) model, allowing management of student ranking positions.\\n\\n`ItemAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `Item` model, allowing management of items that can be ordered by students.\\n\\n`PedidoAdmin`: Configures the Django admin interface for the `Pedido` (order) model, allowing management of student orders for items.\",\"/jiujitsu_academy/wsgi.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a WSGI (Web Server Gateway Interface) configuration file for the Django web framework. The purpose of this file is to set up the WSGI application for the \\\"jiujitsu_academy\\\" project, which is the main entry point for the web application. It loads the Django settings module and returns the WSGI application object, which can be used by a web server to serve the Django application.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/aluno/presencas.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a list of attendance requests for a student in an academic Jiu-Jitsu academy. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather, it uses Django template tags and HTML to render the content.\\n\\nThe file extends the `base.html` template and defines the following blocks:\\n\\n`title`: Displays the page title \\\"Minhas Presenças - Academia de Jiu-Jitsu\\\".\\n`content`: Renders the main content of the page, which includes a table of attendance requests, a button to create a new request, and a link to go back to the dashboard.\\n\\nThe template checks if there are any attendance requests and displays them in a table, showing the date, class, status, rejection reason (if applicable), and an option to cancel the request if it\\u0027s pending. If there are no attendance requests, a message is displayed.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/ranking_form.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template for a new ranking page in an academia (academy) of Jiu-Jitsu. It extends the base template and provides a form for creating a new ranking, allowing the user to specify the title, type, class, start and end dates, and whether the ranking is active. The template also includes options for selecting the type of ranking (Menos Faltas, Mini-Campeonato, or Aluno Destaque) and the class (if applicable).\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/graduacoes.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a list of students and their respective belt ranks and grades. It extends the base template and uses custom tags from the `academia_tags` module. The template includes a table that displays the student\\u0027s name, belt rank, grade, and graduation date, as well as an \\\"Edit\\\" button to update the student\\u0027s information.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/item_confirm_delete.html\":\"This file is a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a confirmation page for deleting an item. It does not contain any classes or functions. The template displays a message asking the user to confirm the deletion of an item, and provides two buttons: one to confirm the deletion and one to cancel the operation.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/change_user_type.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a form for changing a user\\u0027s type from \\\"Aluno\\\" (Student) to \\\"Professor\\\" (Teacher). It includes error handling and a confirmation step that requires the admin user\\u0027s password.\\n\\nThere are no classes or functions defined in this file. The file\\u0027s main purpose is to render a template for the \\\"Change User Type\\\" functionality in the Academia de Jiu-Jitsu web application.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/login.html\":\"This file is a Django template for a login page. It includes a form for users to enter their username and password, and a link to request access to the system. The template uses Bootstrap for styling and includes error handling for invalid login attempts.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/presencas.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a page for managing attendance requests in an academic jiu-jitsu academy. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather, it uses Django template tags and HTML to render the page.\\n\\nThe main functionality of this file is:\\n\\n1. Displaying a table of pending attendance requests, including the student\\u0027s name, class, and attendance date.\\n2. Providing buttons to approve or reject each attendance request.\\n3. Displaying a modal dialog for rejecting an attendance request, allowing the user to provide a reason for the rejection.\\n4. Providing a link to return to the dashboard.\",\"/academia/migrations/0003_item_remove_pedido_descricao_remove_pedido_tipo_and_more.py\":\"This file appears to be a Django migration file that makes changes to the `academia` app\\u0027s models. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the following model changes:\\n\\n`Migration`: This migration creates a new `Item` model with fields for name, type, value, and quantity, and also makes changes to the `Pedido` model, including removing the `descricao` and `tipo` fields and adding a `quantidade` field and a foreign key to the `Item` model.\\n\\n`Graduacao`: The `faixa` field in the `Graduacao` model is updated to include more choices for the belt color.\\n\\nIn summary, this migration file introduces a new `Item` model and makes changes to the existing `Pedido` and `Graduacao` models in the `academia` app.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/itens.html\":\"This file is a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a view for managing items in an academy of Jiu-Jitsu. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather, it displays a table of items with their details and provides buttons for creating, editing, and deleting items. The template also includes a link to the dashboard page.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/item_form.html\":\"This file is a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a form for creating or editing an item in an academia de Jiu-Jitsu (Jiu-Jitsu academy) application.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it uses Django template tags and variables to dynamically generate the HTML content based on the form instance and the user\\u0027s actions (creating a new item or editing an existing one).\",\"/templates/list_attendance_requests.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that displays a list of pending attendance requests and processed attendance requests. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather uses Django template tags and HTML to render the content.\\n\\nThe file performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Displays a list of pending attendance requests, including the student\\u0027s photo, name, class, and the date of the attendance request. It also provides a button to \\\"Analize\\\" each pending request.\\n2. Displays a list of processed attendance requests, including the student\\u0027s photo, name, class, the date of the attendance request, and the status of the request (approved or denied).\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/relatorios.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a report page for a Jiu-Jitsu academy. It allows the user to select the type of report they want to generate, such as classes, attendance, students, promotions, rankings, or requests, and the time period they want to view. The template then displays the relevant statistics and data in a tabular format, with options to download the report in PDF or XLSX format.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a Django template that uses HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to create the user interface and handle the report generation functionality.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/aluno/pedidos.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a list of orders made by a student in an academy. It extends the base template and includes the following:\\n\\n`base.html`: The base template that provides the overall structure and layout for the page.\\n\\n`title`: The title of the page, which is \\\"Meus Pedidos - Academia de Jiu-Jitsu\\\".\\n\\n`content`: The main content of the page, which includes a table displaying the student\\u0027s orders, including the item, quantity, status, and date of the request. The page also includes a button to create a new order and a button to go back to the dashboard.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/perfil.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that displays a user\\u0027s profile information and allows the user to change their password. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather uses Django template tags and HTML to render the page.\\n\\nThe main sections of the template are:\\n\\n`{% extends \\u0027academia/base.html\\u0027 %}`: Extends the base template for the Academia website.\\n`{% block title %}`: Sets the title of the page.\\n`{% block content %}`: Defines the main content of the page.\\n`{% if messages %}`: Displays any messages (e.g., success or error messages) for the user.\\n`\\u003cdiv class\\u003d\\\"accordion\\\" id\\u003d\\\"accordionPerfil\\\"\\u003e`: Creates an accordion-style interface with two sections: \\\"Dados do Perfil\\\" (Profile Data) and \\\"Alterar Senha\\\" (Change Password).\\n`{% if user.photo %}`: Displays the user\\u0027s profile photo, if available.\\n`{% if graduacao %}`: Displays the user\\u0027s graduation information, if available.\\n`\\u003cform method\\u003d\\\"post\\\" action\\u003d\\\"{% url \\u0027perfil\\u0027 %}\\\"\\u003e`: Handles the password change form submission.\\n\\nOverall, this template provides a user-friendly interface for users to view and update their profile information, as well as change their password, within the context of the Academia website.\",\"/academia/views.py\":\"This file contains the views for a Django web application for an academic institution. It includes functionality for students, professors, and administrators to manage attendance, graduations, lesson plans, rankings, and orders. The file does not contain any classes, but rather a collection of functions that handle various aspects of the application\\u0027s functionality.\\n\\n`index`: Renders the homepage, displaying lists of students and professors for unauthenticated users.\\n\\n`login_view`: Handles user authentication and login.\\n\\n`logout_view`: Logs out the current user.\\n\\n`solicitar_acesso`: Handles the process of requesting access to the application.\\n\\n`dashboard`: Renders the appropriate dashboard view based on the user\\u0027s role (student, professor, or administrator).\\n\\n`perfil`: Renders the user\\u0027s profile page.\\n\\n`perfil_editar`: Allows the user to edit their profile information.\\n\\n`aluno_marcar_presenca`: Allows a student to request attendance for a class.\\n\\n`aluno_cancelar_presenca`: Allows a student to cancel a pending attendance request.\\n\\n`aluno_presencas`: Displays a student\\u0027s attendance requests.\\n\\n`aluno_graduacoes`: Displays a student\\u0027s graduations.\\n\\n`aluno_pedidos`: Displays a student\\u0027s orders.\\n\\n`aluno_pedido_novo`: Allows a student to create a new order.\\n\\n`aluno_relatorios`: Renders the student\\u0027s reports page.\\n\\n`professor_turmas`: Displays the professor\\u0027s classes.\\n\\n`professor_turma_nova`: Renders the form for creating a new class.\\n\\n`professor_turma_editar`: Renders the form for editing a class.\\n\\n`professor_alunos`: Displays the list of students.\\n\\n`professor_aluno_desativar`: Deactivates a student.\\n\\n`professor_aluno_ativar`: Activates a student.\\n\\n`professor_aluno_definir_tipo`: Allows a professor or administrator to change a student\\u0027s role to professor.\\n\\n`professor_presencas`: Displays the pending attendance requests for the professor\\u0027s classes.\\n\\n`professor_presenca_aprovar`: Approves a pending attendance request.\\n\\n`professor_presenca_rejeitar`: Rejects a pending attendance request.\\n\\n`professor_graduacoes`: Displays the graduations for the students in the professor\\u0027s classes.\\n\\n`professor_graduacao_editar`: Allows a professor to edit a student\\u0027s graduation.\\n\\n`professor_planos_aula`: Displays the lesson plans for the professor\\u0027s classes.\\n\\n`professor_plano_aula_novo`: Renders the form for creating a new lesson plan.\\n\\n`professor_plano_aula_editar`: Renders the form for editing a lesson plan.\\n\\n`professor_rankings`: Displays the rankings.\\n\\n`professor_ranking_novo`: Renders the form for creating a new ranking.\\n\\n`professor_pedidos`: Displays the orders for the professor\\u0027s students.\\n\\n`professor_pedido_aprovar`: Approves a student\\u0027s order.\\n\\n`professor_pedido_rejeitar`: Rejects a student\\u0027s order.\\n\\n`professor_pedido_cancelar`: Cancels a student\\u0027s order.\\n\\n`professor_pedido_entregar`: Marks a student\\u0027s order as delivered.\\n\\n`professor_itens`: Displays the available items.\\n\\n`professor_item_novo`: Renders the form for creating a new item.\\n\\n`professor_item_editar`: Renders the form for editing an item.\\n\\n`professor_item_deletar`: Deletes an item.\\n\\n`relatorio_pedidos`: Generates a report of student orders based on various filters.\",\"/academia/migrations/0005_alter_pedido_status.py\":\"This file appears to be a Django migration file that alters the `status` field of the `Pedido` model in the `academia` app.\\n\\n`Migration`: This class represents a Django migration that updates the `status` field of the `Pedido` model.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions. It only defines a migration that changes the choices available for the `status` field of the `Pedido` model, adding new statuses such as \\\"Em fila\\\", \\\"Aprovado\\\", \\\"Cancelado\\\", \\\"Rejeitado\\\", and \\\"Entregue\\\".\",\"/academia/templates/academia/dashboard_administrador.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides the content for the administrator\\u0027s dashboard page. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather displays various information and statistics related to the academy\\u0027s management, such as the number of active classes, pending attendance requests, active and pending students, graduation management, lesson plans, rankings, orders, and reports. The template also includes a section to display the birthdays of the month.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/planos_aula.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a list of lesson plans for a Jiu-Jitsu academy. It extends the base template and includes a table that shows the title, class, start date, end date, status, and actions for each lesson plan. The template also includes a button to create a new lesson plan and a button to go back to the dashboard.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/aluno/pedido_form.html\":\"This file is a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a form for creating a new order in an academia (martial arts school) application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the structure and content of the HTML page for the \\\"New Order\\\" view.\\n\\nThe template includes the following sections:\\n\\n`{% extends \\u0027academia/base.html\\u0027 %}`: This line extends the `base.html` template, which likely contains the common layout and structure for all pages in the application.\\n\\n`{% block title %}`: This block sets the title of the page to \\\"Novo Pedido - Academia de Jiu-Jitsu\\\".\\n\\n`{% block content %}`: This block contains the main content of the page, which includes a card with a form for creating a new order.\\n\\nThe form includes a CSRF token, the form fields, and two buttons: one to submit the form and create the order, and another to cancel the operation and return to the \\\"Aluno Pedidos\\\" page.\",\"/academia/urls.py\":\"This file defines the URL patterns for the Django application \\\"academia\\\". It maps different URLs to corresponding views (functions) that handle the application\\u0027s functionality. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`index`: Handles the home/index page of the application.\\n\\n`login_view`: Handles the login functionality.\\n\\n`logout_view`: Handles the logout functionality.\\n\\n`solicitar_acesso`: Handles the request for access to the application.\\n\\n`dashboard`: Handles the dashboard page.\\n\\n`perfil`: Handles the user\\u0027s profile page.\\n\\n`perfil_editar`: Handles the editing of the user\\u0027s profile.\\n\\n`aluno_marcar_presenca`: Handles the marking of attendance by a student.\\n\\n`aluno_cancelar_presenca`: Handles the cancellation of attendance by a student.\\n\\n`aluno_presencas`: Handles the display of a student\\u0027s attendance records.\\n\\n`aluno_relatorios`: Handles the display of a student\\u0027s reports.\\n\\n`aluno_graduacoes`: Handles the display of a student\\u0027s graduations.\\n\\n`aluno_pedidos`: Handles the display of a student\\u0027s requests.\\n\\n`aluno_pedido_novo`: Handles the creation of a new request by a student.\\n\\n`professor_turmas`: Handles the display of a professor\\u0027s classes.\\n\\n`professor_turma_nova`: Handles the creation of a new class by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_turma_editar`: Handles the editing of a class by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_alunos`: Handles the display of a professor\\u0027s students.\\n\\n`professor_aluno_desativar`: Handles the deactivation of a student by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_aluno_ativar`: Handles the activation of a student by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_aluno_definir_tipo`: Handles the setting of a student\\u0027s type by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_presencas`: Handles the display of attendance records for a professor\\u0027s classes.\\n\\n`professor_presenca_aprovar`: Handles the approval of an attendance record by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_presenca_rejeitar`: Handles the rejection of an attendance record by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_graduacoes`: Handles the display of graduations for a professor\\u0027s students.\\n\\n`professor_graduacao_editar`: Handles the editing of a student\\u0027s graduation by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_itens`: Handles the display of items for a professor.\\n\\n`professor_item_novo`: Handles the creation of a new item by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_item_editar`: Handles the editing of an item by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_item_deletar`: Handles the deletion of an item by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_pedidos`: Handles the display of requests for a professor.\\n\\n`professor_pedido_aprovar`: Handles the approval of a request by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_pedido_rejeitar`: Handles the rejection of a request by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_pedido_entregar`: Handles the delivery of a request by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_planos_aula`: Handles the display of lesson plans for a professor.\\n\\n`professor_plano_aula_novo`: Handles the creation of a new lesson plan by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_plano_aula_editar`: Handles the editing of a lesson plan by a professor.\\n\\n`professor_rankings`: Handles the display of rankings for a professor.\\n\\n`professor_ranking_novo`: Handles the creation of a new ranking by a professor.\\n\\n`relatorio_pedidos`: Handles the display of a report for requests.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/aluno_definir_tipo.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a form for defining the user type (student, professor, or administrator) for a specific student. The template includes an alert message, a dropdown menu for selecting the user type, and a password input field for confirming the action. The template also includes navigation buttons to go back and to confirm the changes.\\n\\nThere are no classes or functions defined in this file. The file is responsible for rendering the user type definition form and handling the form submission.\",\"/academia/migrations/0002_alter_ranking_data_fim_attendancerequest.py\":\"This file appears to be a Django migration file that introduces a new model called `AttendanceRequest` and modifies an existing model called `Ranking`.\\n\\n`Migration`: This class represents a Django migration that applies changes to the database schema.\\n\\n`AttendanceRequest`: This model represents a request for attendance, with fields for the attendance date, reason, status, rejection reason, and information about the student and the class (turma) the request is associated with.\\n\\n`Ranking`: The existing `Ranking` model has been modified to allow the `data_fim` (end date) field to be nullable and blank.\",\"/academia/context_processors.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`pending_tasks_processor`: This function checks the user\\u0027s authentication status and role (student, professor, or admin) to determine the pending tasks that should be displayed in the user\\u0027s notifications.\",\"/jiujitsu_academy/urls.py\":\"This file is the URL configuration for the `jiujitsu_academy` Django project. It defines the URL patterns for the project, including the admin site and the `academia` app. If the project is in debug mode, it also configures the serving of media files.\\n\\n`urlpatterns`: A list of URL patterns for the project, including the admin site and the `academia` app.\\n\\n`static()`: A function that configures the serving of media files in debug mode.\",\"/academia/templatetags/academia_tags.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`format_grau`: A function that takes a numerical value and returns a string representation of the value as \\\"Nenhum Grau\\\", \\\"1 Grau\\\", or \\\"{value} Graus\\\" depending on the input.\\n\\n`lookup`: A function that takes a dictionary and a key, and returns the value associated with that key in the dictionary.\\n\\nThis file is a Django template library that provides custom template filters for formatting numerical values and looking up values in a dictionary.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/aluno/marcar_presenca.html\":\"This file is a Django template that allows users to mark their attendance for a specific class. It extends the base template and includes a form where users can select a class and add multiple dates for their attendance. The selected dates are displayed in a list, and users can remove them if needed. When the form is submitted, the selected dates are sent to the server.\\n\\nThere are no classes defined in this file, but there are the following functions:\\n\\n`document.addEventListener(\\u0027DOMContentLoaded\\u0027, function() { ... })`: This function is executed when the HTML document is fully loaded and parsed, and it sets up the event listeners and functions for handling the attendance marking process.\\n\\n`updateSelectedDates()`: This function updates the list of selected dates and the hidden input fields that will be submitted with the form.\",\"/academia/migrations/0006_alter_pedido_status.py\":\"This file appears to be a Django migration file that alters the `status` field of the `Pedido` model in the `academia` app.\\n\\n`Migration`: This class represents a Django migration that updates the `status` field of the `Pedido` model, adding new choices for the status.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, but it does define the following choices for the `status` field:\\n\\n- `PEND`: Pendente (Pending)\\n- `APRO`: Aprovado (Approved)\\n- `CANC`: Cancelado (Canceled)\\n- `REJE`: Rejeitado (Rejected)\\n- `ENTR`: Aguardando entrega (Awaiting delivery)\\n- `FINA`: Finalizado (Completed)\\n\\nThe default status is set to `PEND` (Pending).\",\"/academia/models.py\":\"This file defines the models for a Django application related to a martial arts academy. It includes the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`User`: A custom user model that extends the `AbstractUser` model and includes additional fields such as birthday, group role, and profile photo.\\n\\n`Turma`: A model for representing classes or groups in the academy, with fields for the class name, description, professor, and enrolled students.\\n\\n`TurmaAluno`: An intermediate model for the many-to-many relationship between `Turma` and `User`, representing a student\\u0027s enrollment in a class.\\n\\n`AttendanceRequest`: A model for handling attendance requests from students, with fields for the student, class, attendance date, reason, status, and processing information.\\n\\n`Presenca`: A model for tracking student attendance, with fields for the student, class, attendance date, status, and approval information.\\n\\n`Graduacao`: A model for managing student graduations, with fields for the student, belt color, and degree.\\n\\n`PlanoAula`: A model for creating and managing lesson plans, with fields for the title, description, professor, start and end dates, and associated class.\\n\\n`ItemPlanoAula`: A model for individual items within a lesson plan, with fields for the subject, lesson date, and order.\\n\\n`Ranking`: A model for managing various types of rankings, such as least absences, mini-tournaments, and student of the month, with fields for the title, type, associated class, start and end dates, and active status.\\n\\n`PosicaoRanking`: A model for storing the positions and scores of students in a ranking.\\n\\n`Item`: A model for representing different types of items (e.g., kimono, belt, rashguard, fees) that can be ordered by students.\\n\\n`Pedido`: A model for handling student orders, with fields for the student, item, quantity, status, approval information, and final value.\\n\\n`photo_upload_to`: A function that generates a unique filename for profile photos based on the user\\u0027s ID and the current timestamp.\",\"/pyrightconfig.json\":\"This file appears to be a configuration file for a type checking tool, likely Mypy. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather specifies settings for the type checking process. The key points are:\\n\\n- `reportMissingImports`: Enables reporting of missing imports\\n- `reportMissingTypeStubs`: Disables reporting of missing type stubs\\n- `pythonVersion`: Sets the Python version to 3.10\\n- `typeCheckingMode`: Sets the type checking mode to \\\"basic\\\"\\n- `reportAttributeAccessIssue`: Disables reporting of attribute access issues\\n- `executionEnvironments`: Specifies the root directory and Python executable to use\\n- `include`: Specifies the directories to include in the type checking process, in this case \\\"academia\\\" and \\\"jiujitsu_academy\\\"\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to configure the type checking tool to perform basic type checking on the specified directories, with certain settings to control the behavior of the type checker.\",\"/academia/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided file. The file appears to be an empty XML document, which is a markup language used to structure and organize data. Without any content, the file does not perform any specific functionality.\",\"/templates/process_attendance_request.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that extends the \\u0027base.html\\u0027 template and provides a view for analyzing a student\\u0027s request for attendance. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather displays the student\\u0027s information, allows the user to approve or reject the request, and provides a textarea for entering a rejection reason if necessary.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/turmas.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a list of classes (or \\\"turmas\\\" in Portuguese) in a table format. It allows the user to view the details of each class, such as the name, professor, status, and creation date. The user can also create a new class or edit an existing one.\\n\\n`base.html`: This is the base template that the current template extends, providing a common structure and layout for the web page.\\n\\n`title`: This block sets the title of the web page.\\n\\n`content`: This block contains the main content of the web page, which is the class management section.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it uses Django template tags and variables to display the data and provide functionality for managing classes.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/base.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that serves as the base layout for a web application. It includes a navigation bar, a toast notification system, and a container for displaying content. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the overall structure and functionality of the web application.\\n\\nThe main components of the file are:\\n\\n`\\u003cdocument\\u003e`: This is the root element of the HTML document.\\n`\\u003chead\\u003e`: This section contains the metadata and links to external CSS and JavaScript files.\\n`\\u003cbody\\u003e`: This section contains the main content of the web page, including the navigation bar, toast notifications, and a container for displaying content.\\n`{% block title %}`: This is a Django template tag that allows for the title of the page to be dynamically set.\\n`{% block content %}`: This is a Django template tag that allows for the main content of the page to be dynamically set.\\n`{% block extra_css %}`: This is a Django template tag that allows for additional CSS to be included on the page.\\n`{% block extra_js %}`: This is a Django template tag that allows for additional JavaScript to be included on the page.\\n`{% if user.is_authenticated %}`: This is a Django template tag that checks if the user is authenticated and displays the navigation bar accordingly.\\n`{% if user.is_student or user.is_professor_or_admin %}`: This is a Django template tag that checks the user\\u0027s role and displays the appropriate menu items in the navigation bar.\\n`{% if messages %}`: This is a Django template tag that displays any messages that have been added to the request.\\n\\nOverall, this file serves as the base layout for a web application that provides functionality for students, professors, and administrators to manage various aspects of an academic program.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/perfil_editar.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a form for users to edit their profile information. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the structure and content of the HTML page.\\n\\nThe main components of the file are:\\n\\n`{% extends \\u0027academia/base.html\\u0027 %}`: This line extends the `base.html` template, which likely contains the common structure and layout for the website.\\n\\n`{% block title %}`: This block sets the title of the page to \\\"Editar Perfil - Academia de Jiu-Jitsu\\\".\\n\\n`{% block content %}`: This block contains the main content of the page, which is a form for editing the user\\u0027s profile information, including their name, email, birthday, and profile photo.\\n\\nThe form includes input fields for the user\\u0027s first name, last name, email, birthday, and profile photo, as well as buttons to save the changes or cancel the operation.\",\"/manage.py\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`main()`: This function sets the Django settings module, imports the necessary Django management functions, and executes the Django command-line utility for administrative tasks.\\n\\nThe file serves as the entry point for Django\\u0027s command-line utility, allowing users to run administrative tasks such as starting the development server, creating database migrations, and managing the application\\u0027s configuration.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/pedidos.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a table of orders and provides functionality for professors to manage those orders. It includes modals for approving, rejecting, canceling, and delivering orders.\\n\\nThere are no classes defined in this file. The following functions are present:\\n\\n`professor_pedido_rejeitar`: Handles the rejection of an order by a professor.\\n`professor_pedido_cancelar`: Handles the cancellation of an order by a professor.\\n`professor_pedido_aprovar`: Handles the approval of an order by a professor.\\n`professor_pedido_entregar`: Handles the delivery of an order by a professor.\",\"/academia/migrations/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided document. The document appears to be an empty XML document, which does not contain any meaningful content.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/plano_aula_form.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a form for creating or editing a lesson plan. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the structure and content of the HTML page.\\n\\nThe template includes the following sections:\\n\\n`{% block title %}`: Displays the title of the page, which is either \\\"Editar Plano de Aula\\\" or \\\"Novo Plano de Aula\\\" depending on whether a lesson plan is being edited or created.\\n\\n`{% block content %}`: Defines the main content of the page, which includes a form with fields for the lesson plan\\u0027s title, description, class, start date, end date, and active status. The form also includes buttons for saving the lesson plan or canceling the operation.\\n\\nOverall, this file is responsible for rendering the HTML page for creating or editing a lesson plan in the context of a Jiu-Jitsu academy.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/dashboard_professor.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a dashboard view for a professor in an academic Jiu-Jitsu academy. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather displays various information and management options for the professor, such as managing classes, attendance, students, graduations, lesson plans, rankings, orders, and reports. The template also includes a section for displaying the birthdays of students for the selected month.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/alunos.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a list of students in a jiu-jitsu academy. It extends the base template and includes a table with student information, such as their name, email, and status (active, inactive, or pending). The template also includes buttons to activate, deactivate, and change the type of a student.\\n\\nThere are no classes defined in this file. The following functions are present:\\n\\n`professor_aluno_ativar`: Activates a student.\\n`professor_aluno_desativar`: Deactivates a student.\\n`professor_aluno_definir_tipo`: Changes the type of a student.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/turma_incluir_alunos.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that allows a professor to add students to a class. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather a series of HTML blocks and JavaScript code to handle the functionality of the page.\\n\\nThe main components of the file are:\\n\\n`{% extends \\u0027academia/base.html\\u0027 %}`: Extends the base template for the Academia de Jiu-Jitsu website.\\n\\n`{% block title %}`: Sets the title of the page.\\n\\n`{% block content %}`: Defines the main content of the page, which includes a form to add new students to the class and a list of students currently in the class.\\n\\n`{% block extra_js %}`: Includes additional JavaScript code to handle the filtering and selection of students in the form.\\n\\nThe file provides a user interface for professors to manage the students in their classes, allowing them to add new students and view the current roster of students and their status (approved, pending, or rejected).\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/presenca_rejeitar.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template for a page that allows a professor to reject a student\\u0027s attendance. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather a template structure that extends a base template and defines the content for the \\\"Rejeitar Presença\\\" (Reject Attendance) page.\\n\\nThe template includes the following:\\n\\n`{% extends \\u0027academia/base.html\\u0027 %}`: Extends the base template for the Academia de Jiu-Jitsu website.\\n\\n`{% block title %}`: Defines the title of the page as \\\"Rejeitar Presença - Academia de Jiu-Jitsu\\\".\\n\\n`{% block content %}`: Defines the content of the page, which includes displaying the student\\u0027s name, class, and attendance date, as well as a form for the professor to enter a reason for rejecting the attendance.\\n\\nThe form includes a CSRF token and a textarea field for the professor to enter the reason for rejecting the attendance. The template also includes buttons for submitting the form and canceling the rejection.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/turma_solicitacoes.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a list of class requests for a specific class in an Jiu-Jitsu academy. It allows the professor to approve or reject the requests.\\n\\nThere are no classes defined in this file. The following functions are defined:\\n\\n`professor_solicitacao_aprovar`: Allows the professor to approve a class request.\\n`professor_solicitacao_rejeitar`: Allows the professor to reject a class request.\",\"/jiujitsu_academy/settings.py\":\"This file is a Django settings file for the \\\"jiujitsu_academy\\\" project. It configures various settings for the Django application, including the database, middleware, templates, and static/media file handling. It also sets up the custom user model and login/logout functionality.\\n\\n`Path`: A class from the `pathlib` module used to build paths inside the project.\\n\\n`INSTALLED_APPS`: A list of Django applications installed in the project.\\n\\n`MIDDLEWARE`: A list of middleware classes used in the project.\\n\\n`ROOT_URLCONF`: The root URL configuration for the project.\\n\\n`TEMPLATES`: The configuration for the Django template engine.\\n\\n`WSGI_APPLICATION`: The WSGI application entry point for the project.\\n\\n`DATABASES`: The configuration for the project\\u0027s database.\\n\\n`AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS`: The password validation rules for the project.\\n\\n`LANGUAGE_CODE`, `TIME_ZONE`, `USE_I18N`, `USE_TZ`: The internationalization and localization settings for the project.\\n\\n`STATIC_URL`, `STATIC_ROOT`, `MEDIA_URL`, `MEDIA_ROOT`: The configuration for serving static and media files in the project.\\n\\n`DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD`: The default primary key field type for the project.\\n\\n`AUTH_USER_MODEL`: The custom user model used in the project.\\n\\n`LOGIN_URL`, `LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL`: The login and redirect URLs for the project.\",\"/academia/migrations/0004_alter_pedido_status.py\":\"This file appears to be a Django migration file that modifies the `status` field of the `Pedido` model in the `academia` app.\\n\\n`Migration`: This class represents a Django migration that alters the `status` field of the `Pedido` model, adding new choices for the status of the order.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/graduacao_form.html\":\"This file is a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides a form for editing a student\\u0027s graduation in an Jiu-Jitsu academy. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather, it defines the structure and content of the HTML page that will be rendered when the user requests to edit a student\\u0027s graduation.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/professor/turma_form.html\":\"This file is a Django template that displays a form for creating or editing a class (turma) in an academic jiu-jitsu program. It extends the base template and includes blocks for the title and content.\\n\\nThere are no classes defined in this file. The following functions are present:\\n\\n`block title`: Displays the title of the page, which is either \\\"Editar Turma\\\" or \\\"Nova Turma\\\" depending on whether a class object is passed to the template.\\n\\n`block content`: Renders the form for creating or editing a class, including fields for the class name, description, and whether the class is active.\",\"/academia/templatetags/__init__.py\":\"There are no classes or functions in the provided file. The file appears to be an empty XML document with a `\\u003cdocument\\u003e` tag, but no content within it.\",\"/jiujitsu_academy/asgi.py\":\"This file is the ASGI (Asynchronous Server Gateway Interface) configuration for the \\\"jiujitsu_academy\\\" Django project. It sets the Django settings module and retrieves the ASGI application.\\n\\n`get_asgi_application()`: This function retrieves the ASGI application for the Django project.\\n\\n`application`: This is the ASGI application for the \\\"jiujitsu_academy\\\" Django project.\",\"/academia/migrations/0001_initial.py\":\"This file appears to be a Django migration file that defines the database models for an academic management system. It does not contain any functions, but rather defines the following classes:\\n\\n`User`: A custom user model that extends the default Django user model with additional fields such as birthday, group role, and profile photo.\\n\\n`Graduacao`: A model that represents a student\\u0027s graduation level, including their belt color and degree.\\n\\n`Pedido`: A model that represents a request made by a student, such as for a kimono, belt, or exam.\\n\\n`PlanoAula`: A model that represents a lesson plan, including its title, description, start and end dates, and the professor who created it.\\n\\n`ItemPlanoAula`: A model that represents an individual item within a lesson plan, including the subject and date of the lesson.\\n\\n`Turma`: A model that represents a class, including its name, description, and the professor who teaches it.\\n\\n`TurmaAluno`: A model that represents a student\\u0027s enrollment in a class, including the status of their enrollment.\\n\\n`Ranking`: A model that represents a ranking or competition, including its title, type, start and end dates, and the class it is associated with.\\n\\n`PosicaoRanking`: A model that represents a student\\u0027s position in a ranking, including their ranking and score.\\n\\n`Presenca`: A model that represents a student\\u0027s attendance in a class, including the date, status, and the professor who approved or rejected the attendance.\",\"/academia/templates/academia/dashboard_aluno.html\":\"This file appears to be a Django template that extends the `base.html` template and provides the content for the student dashboard page. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather displays various sections of information for the student, including:\\n\\n1. A greeting message with the student\\u0027s name and a logout link.\\n2. A dashboard with three cards: \\\"Mark Attendance\\\", \\\"My Attendance\\\", and \\\"Reports\\\".\\n3. A list of the student\\u0027s enrolled classes.\\n4. A section displaying the birthdays of other students in the current month.\\n\\nThe template uses Django\\u0027s template language to dynamically generate the content based on the data passed from the view.\",\"/README.md\":\"This file appears to be a documentation for a Python Django project that manages a Jiu-Jitsu academy. It does not contain any class or function definitions, but rather provides an overview of the system\\u0027s features and installation instructions.\\n\\nThe file describes the following features of the system:\\n\\n1. **User Roles and Permissions**: The system has three user roles: Student (STD), Professor (PRO), and Administrator (ADM), each with different levels of access.\\n2. **Student Panel**: Allows students to mark attendance, cancel requests, view attendance status, generate reports, and manage their profile.\\n3. **Professor Panel**: Allows professors to manage classes, approve/reject student registrations, deactivate students, manage attendance, control student graduations, create lesson plans, manage rankings, handle orders, and generate reports.\\n4. **Installation**: Provides instructions for installing the project dependencies, running migrations, creating a superuser, and starting the server.\\n5. **Project Structure**: Outlines the structure of the Django project, including the main application folder, models, views, templates, and admin configuration.\\n6. **Database**: Mentions that the system uses SQLite as the default database.\\n7. **Observations**: Provides additional information about the use of Bootstrap 5 for the frontend and the handling of user profile photos.\"}"}
